params.name: created_at
params.description: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to filter by the created at timestamp. For example, for all times after Jan 1 2000 12:00 UTC, use created_at%5Bgt%5D=2000-01-01T12:00:00Z.

params.name: updated_at
params.description: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to filter by the updated at timestamp. For example, for all times after Jan 1 2000 12:00 UTC, use updated_at%5Bgt%5D=2000-01-01T12:00:00Z.

params.name: id[]
params.description: If you have specific IDs to retrieve in bulk, you can pass them as query parameters delimited with `id[]=`, for example `?id[]=123&id[]=abc`.

params.name: name[]
params.description: If you have specific names to retrieve in bulk, you can pass them as query parameters delimited with `name[]=`, for example `?name[]=123&name[]=abc`.

params.name: balances
params.description: Use `balances[effective_at_lower_bound]` and `balances[effective_at_upper_bound]` to get the balances change between the two timestamps. The lower bound is inclusive while the upper bound is exclusive of the provided timestamps. If no value is supplied the balances will be retrieved not including that bound.

params.name: pending_balance_amount
params.description: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), `eq` (=), or `not_eq` (!=) to filter by balance amount.

params.name: posted_balance_amount
params.description: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), `eq` (=), or `not_eq` (!=) to filter by balance amount.

params.name: available_balance_amount
params.description: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), `eq` (=), or `not_eq` (!=) to filter by balance amount.

params.name: created_at
params.description: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to filter by the created at timestamp. For example, for all times after Jan 1 2000 12:00 UTC, use created_at%5Bgt%5D=2000-01-01T12:00:00Z.

params.name: updated_at
params.description: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to filter by the updated at timestamp. For example, for all times after Jan 1 2000 12:00 UTC, use updated_at%5Bgt%5D=2000-01-01T12:00:00Z.

params.name: balances
params.description: Use `balances[effective_at_lower_bound]` and `balances[effective_at_upper_bound]` to get the balances change between the two timestamps. The lower bound is inclusive while the upper bound is exclusive of the provided timestamps. If no value is supplied the balances will be retrieved not including that bound. Use `balances[as_of_lock_version]` to retrieve a balance as of a specific Ledger Account `lock_version`.

params.description: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to filter by the transaction's effective date. Format YYYY-MM-DD

params.name: effective_at
params.description: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to filter by the transaction's effective time. Format ISO8601

params.name: updated_at
params.description: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to filter by the posted at timestamp. For example, for all times after Jan 1 2000 12:00 UTC, use updated_at%5Bgt%5D=2000-01-01T12:00:00Z.

params.name: as_of_lock_version
params.description: Shows all ledger entries that were present on a ledger account at a particular `lock_version`. You must also specify `ledger_account_id`.

params.name: ledger_account_lock_version
params.description: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to filter by the lock_version of a ledger account. For example, for all entries created at or before before lock_version 1000 of a ledger account, use `ledger_account_lock_version%5Blte%5D=1000`.

params.name: ledger_account_category_id
params.description: Get all ledger entries that match the direction specified. One of `credit`, `debit`.

params.name: ledger_account_statement_id
params.description: Get all ledger entries that are included in the ledger account statement.

params.name: show_deleted
params.description: If true, response will include ledger entries that were deleted. When you update a ledger transaction to specify a new set of entries, the previous entries are deleted.

params.name: direction
params.description: If true, response will include ledger entries that were deleted. When you update a ledger transaction to specify a new set of entries, the previous entries are deleted.

params.name: status
params.description: Get all ledger entries that match the status specified. One of `pending`, `posted`, or `archived`. For multiple statuses, use `status[]=pending&status[]=posted`.

params.name: status[]
params.description: Get all ledger entries that match the status specified. One of `pending`, `posted`, or `archived`. For multiple statuses, use `status[]=pending&status[]=posted`.

params.name: order_by
params.description: Order by `created_at` or `effective_at` in `asc` or `desc` order. For example, to order by `effective_at asc`, use `order_by%5Beffective_at%5D=asc`. Ordering by only one field at a time is supported.

params.name: amount
params.description: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to filter by amount.

params.name: show_balances
params.description: If true, response will include the balances attached to the ledger entry. If there is no balance available, null will be returned instead.

params.description: For example, if you want to query for records with metadata key `Type` and value `Loan`, the query would be `metadata%5BType%5D=Loan`. This encodes the query parameters.

params.description: The current status of the incoming payment order. One of `pending`, `completed`, or `returned`.

params.name: type
params.description: One of: `ach`, `book`, `check`, `eft`, `interac`, `rtp`, `sepa`, `signet`, or `wire`.

params.description: Filters incoming payment details with an as_of_date starting on or after the specified date (YYYY-MM-DD).

params.description: Filters incoming payment details with an as_of_date starting on or before the specified date (YYYY-MM-DD).

params.name: virtual_account_id
params.description: If the incoming payment detail is in a virtual account, the ID of the Virtual Account.

params.name: subtype
params.description: An additional layer of classification for the type of incoming payment detail. For example, a `type` of `stablecoin` may have a `subtype` of `ethereum` or `solana`.

params.name: priority
params.description: Either `normal` or `high`. For ACH and EFT payments, `high` represents a same-day ACH or EFT transfer, respectively. For check payments, `high` can mean an overnight check rather than standard mail.
